title: Atlas species config v2
description: Taxonomic identity for a species the atlas knows about. Pure metadata: species_id, label, scientific name, NCBI taxid. NO operational fields (cohort, populations, reference) — those moved to cohort.config and genome.config. Per HIERARCHY_SPEC.md, species is a tag the registry stamps on genomes and samples; nothing operational runs against a species directly.

species_id.description: Unique short identifier. Used as a tag in genome.species_composition[] and sample_master.species. Must be filesystem-safe and stable forever (don't rename).

label.description: Human-readable display name. Used in UI labels and logs.

scientific_name.description: Full Latin binomial. Optional but recommended for unambiguous identification across the manuscript and reference databases.

ncbi_taxid.description: NCBI Taxonomy ID. Optional; useful for cross-referencing external resources.

is_hybrid.description: True for hybrid 'species' tags (e.g. F1_hybrid). The atlas treats hybrid species_ids the same as pure ones — this flag is for documentation and for analyses that want to filter.

parent_species_ids.description: When is_hybrid=true, the parental species_ids. e.g. for F1_hybrid this would be ['gariepinus', 'macrocephalus']. Empty for pure species.

notes.description: Free-form notes about this species: collection regions, taxonomic uncertainties, etc.

hybrid_tag.label: F1 hybrid (C. gariepinus x C. macrocephalus)
hybrid_tag.notes: Used to tag hybrid samples in cohort samples_tsv. The hybrid genome assembly carries species_composition: ['gariepinus', 'macrocephalus']; hybrid SAMPLES carry species: 'F1_hybrid'.
